title: Order-disorder trade-off in dirty quantum systems
abstract: We prove a trade-off theorem for order and disorder parameters in one-dimensional quantum spin systems with quenched disorder. For a disordered ensemble with exact Ising symmetry and average translation symmetry, any gapped ensemble must have one and only one of the following: an $O(1)$ order parameter or an $O(1)$ disorder parameter with even parity, both of the Edwards-Anderson type. The result extends to nearly gapped ensembles that accommodate Griffiths-type rare-region effects. These results offer a powerful and rigorous framework to understand the disorder effects beyond perturbative approaches. As applications, we (1) establish the existence of string order parameters for SPT phases; (2) derive a Lieb-Schultz-Mattis-type constraint for disordered ensembles, which requires a nearly gapped ensemble to spontaneously break the symmetry; and (3) discuss similar trade-off relations for disordered fermion chains, leading to an improved understanding of certain "intrinsically disordered" topological phases.
summary: This work proves a fundamental trade-off theorem stating that a gapped quantum system cannot simultaneously exhibit both long-range order and long-range disorder in the presence of quenched disorder. By analyzing correlation functions and rare-region effects, the authors provide a powerful, rigorous tool to classify disordered quantum phases, extending concepts like the Lieb-Schultz-Mattis theorem to dirty systems.
main_problem: The paper establishes a rigorous trade-off theorem governing the coexistence of order and disorder parameters in gapped quantum spin systems subjected to quenched disorder.
main_result: For gapped ensembles, the system must possess either a non-zero order parameter or a non-zero disorder parameter, but not both; this trade-off extends to nearly gapped regimes.
method: The proof combines probabilistic arguments, utilizing concepts like the Local Perturbation Principle and analyzing the incompatibility of local ordering and disordering events.
model_or_system: The study focuses on one-dimensional quantum spin systems (e.g., Ising chains) with quenched disorder, analyzed via ensemble averages of correlation functions.
key_observables: Order parameter (long-range correlation $\langle O_i O_j angle$) and Disorder parameter (string correlator $\langle O_i O_j \prod S_k angle$).
important_parameters: The gap size ($\epsilon$), the disorder strength, and the decay rate of rare-region probabilities ($p|R|$).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the system is gapped or nearly gapped, and relies on the Local Perturbation Principle (LPPL) for bounding correlations.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the notation for defining local operators on disjoint intervals ($I, I', I_\pm$) used in the rigorous mathematical proof structure.
paper_structure: The paper develops the trade-off theorem by first establishing the necessary conditions for order/disorder parameters in gapped systems, then extending this to nearly gapped regimes, and finally applying the result to derive constraints like the disordered Lieb-Schultz-Mattis theorem.
why_interesting: The rigorous mathematical framework connecting order and disorder parameters provides powerful diagnostic tools for characterizing topological phases in the presence of realistic disorder, which is a major challenge in condensed matter theory.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
